Key Recommendations: Exercise therapy (strengthening and aerobic) is strongly recommended; manual therapy may be used as adjunct; patient education on activity modification and weight management is recommended
Notes: JOSPT Clinical Practice Guidelines for PT Management of Knee OA

Key Recommendations: Early supervised physical therapy emphasizing rotator cuff and scapular stabilization recommended; surgery considered after failure of conservative management at 3–6 months
Notes: AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline on Optimizing Management of Rotator Cuff Problems

Key Recommendations: Active approaches (exercise, manual therapy) preferred over passive modalities; early mobilization recommended; psychosocial factors should be assessed; avoid prolonged rest
Notes: JOSPT CPG on Low Back Pain — consistent with APTA clinical practice guidelines
